the_rabbit_hole_discovery: ^Alice sat on the riverbank, her sister's book offering no solace in the drowsy heat. Suddenly, a White Rabbit with pink eyes, clutching a golden watch, scurried past, muttering of his tardiness. Driven by a burning curiosity, Alice followed him across the field, watching in disbelief as he vanished down a deep rabbit-hole. Without a thought for how she might return, she tumbled in after him, falling down a well so deep that she had time to examine the cupboards and maps lining its walls before landing softly on a heap of dry leaves.
^Explore the long, low hall and try every locked door.
^Drink from the 'DRINK ME' bottle immediately to change your size.
^Ignore the table and run after the White Rabbit deeper into the dark passage.
the_rabbit_hole_discovery_result_1: ^You wander the hall, trying every door, but they are all firmly locked. Your patience is rewarded when you discover a tiny curtain hiding a door only fifteen inches high, which the golden key on the glass table fits perfectly.
the_rabbit_hole_discovery_result_2: ^The bottle tastes of cherry-tart and custard. You shrink to a height of ten inches, perfectly sized to pass through the door, though you realize you have left the key on the table, now far out of reach.
the_rabbit_hole_discovery_result_3: ^You rush blindly into the darkness to keep the Rabbit in sight, but you lose your way in the twisting tunnels and find yourself shivering in a damp, cold corner, unsure of which way leads back to the hall.
the_pool_of_tears: ^Having shrunk to a mere ten inches, you found yourself unable to reach the golden key upon the glass table. In your frustration, you wept such a deluge of tears that a vast pool formed around you, four inches deep and stretching across the hall. As you swam about, trying to find a way out, you encountered a Mouse paddling frantically in the salt water. Fearing it might be offended by talk of your cat Dinah, you sought to be polite, yet the conversation only served to lead you into a crowded assembly of birds and animals upon the shore, all of whom were as wet and uncomfortable as yourself.
^Join the Dodo in the Caucus-race to get dry.
^Listen to the Mouse's long and sad tale to understand his hatred of cats.
^Mention your cat Dinah again to see if it makes the animals move faster.
the_pool_of_tears_result_1: ^You run in a circle with the others until you are perfectly dry. The Dodo declares that everyone has won, and you are presented with your own thimble as a prize, which fills you with a sense of accomplishment.
the_pool_of_tears_result_2: ^The Mouse recounts a complex, shifting history. You listen intently, gaining a deep, empathetic insight into the creature's world, which leaves you feeling remarkably wise and composed despite the strangeness of Wonderland.
the_pool_of_tears_result_3: ^The mere mention of Dinah causes a panic. The birds and animals scatter in terror, leaving you entirely alone in the wood, feeling isolated and quite foolish for having frightened your only companions.
the_caterpillar_and_the_mushroom: ^After escaping the White Rabbit's house—where you had grown to such an immense size that you nearly broke the cottage apart—you fled into a thick wood. There, you encountered a giant, blue Caterpillar perched atop a large mushroom, smoking a hookah with calm detachment. He questioned your very identity, and you found yourself struggling to explain the many bewildering changes you had endured. Before crawling away, he offered a cryptic piece of advice: one side of the mushroom would make you grow taller, while the other would make you grow shorter. Now, you stand before the round, mysterious fungus, holding two fragments in your trembling hands.
^Nibble the right-hand piece to reach a more comfortable height.
^Eat both pieces at once to master your own size.
^Eat the left-hand piece, hoping to grow tall enough to see over the trees.
the_caterpillar_and_the_mushroom_result_: ^You carefully nibble the right-hand piece. You feel your stature settle, and with a sense of relief, you return to your usual height, feeling prepared to finally enter the lovely garden you have sought for so long.

^By balancing both pieces, you gain a strange, magical control over your own form. You shrink and grow with elegance, eventually settling into a perfect size that allows you to walk through the garden gates with grace and confidence.

^You eat the left-hand piece, but instead of growing tall, your neck stretches out like a serpent, twisting through the branches until you are hopelessly tangled and mistaken for a vicious snake by a terrified Pigeon.
the_mad_tea_party: ^Having finally regained your proper height, you find yourself wandering until you come upon a large, peculiar house with chimneys shaped like ears. You soon discover a table set out under a tree, where the March Hare and the Hatter are engaged in a never-ending tea-party, with a very sleepy Dormouse acting as their cushion. The tea-pot is always full, the riddles have no answers, and the conversation is as nonsensical as the time itself, which remains stuck forever at six o'clock. As you approach, they cry out that there is no room, but you boldly seat yourself, eager to see what madness this gathering holds.
^Politely ask the Hatter about his watch and the riddle of the raven.
^Try to solve the Dormouse's riddle about the sisters in the treacle well.
^Insist that the tea-party is a waste of time and demand to leave.
the_mad_tea_party_result_1: ^The Hatter is so delighted by your curiosity that he offers you a fresh cup of tea. You spend a pleasant, if confusing, hour discussing why a raven is like a writing-desk, leaving the party with a sense of amusement.
the_mad_tea_party_result_2: ^You listen so carefully to the Dormouse's tale that you manage to uncover a hidden, logical pattern within the nonsense. The creatures are so impressed by your 'muchness' that they share a secret path to the Queen's garden, making your journey much easier.
the_mad_tea_party_result_3: ^Your lack of patience offends the Hatter, and the March Hare begins throwing bread-and-butter at you. You flee the scene in a hurry, feeling quite disgusted and losing your way in the woods as the sun begins to set.
the_queens_croquet_ground: ^Leaving the madness of the tea-party behind, you finally managed to enter the beautiful garden. However, the serenity was short-lived, for you stumbled upon a scene of utter confusion: the Queen of Hearts was presiding over a croquet game where the mallets were live flamingoes, the balls were hedgehogs, and the arches were soldiers bent double. The air was filled with shouts of 'Off with her head!' and the general terror of the courtiers. You were suddenly called upon to play, and as you stood there, the Cheshire Cat's grin appeared in the air, offering you a brief moment of cynical comfort amidst the royal chaos.
^Try to play the game as fairly as possible, despite the chaos.
^Ask the Cheshire Cat for advice on how to survive the Queen's court.
^Speak up to defend the soldiers when the Queen sentences them.
the_queens_croquet_ground_result_1: ^By focusing on the game and ignoring the Queen's temper, you successfully navigate the ridges and furrows. Your calm demeanor impresses the King, who grants you a temporary reprieve from any royal wrath.
the_queens_croquet_ground_result_2: ^The Cat whispers a secret truth about the Queen's empty threats. Armed with this knowledge, you no longer fear the royal decree, moving through the garden with a newfound, lighthearted confidence that turns the entire court into a mere spectacle for your amusement.
the_queens_croquet_ground_result_3: ^Your sense of justice provokes the Queen. She turns her fury upon you, and you are forced to flee the croquet-ground as a pack of cards rises into the air to chase you down.
the_trial_of_the_knave: ^The final drama unfolds in the courtroom, where the King and Queen of Hearts preside over the trial of the Knave, who stands accused of stealing the royal tarts. The court is a cacophony of birds, beasts, and playing cards, all busily recording nonsensical evidence on their slates. As the White Rabbit reads the absurd verses, you realize you have grown to your full size, and the absurdity of it all—the injustice, the illogical rules, and the Queen's constant cries for execution—wells up within you. You stand in the center of the court, a giant among cardboard puppets, and the Queen, turning purple with rage, shouts the inevitable command: 'Off with her head!'
global decl: ^A curious and resilient child whose evolving size and shifting perspective make her the ultimate explorer of a world that defies physical reality.
